summary: Live Firebase service-account key and hardcoded third-party POS credentials shipped in package
details: The published tarball ships a Google Cloud service-account JSON file (fir-51e77-firebase-adminsdk-x3sdp-fd902b74ae.json) at the package root containing a live RSA private key authenticating as firebase-adminsdk-k7lom@tango-trax.iam.gserviceaccount.com for Firebase project 'tango-trax'. Anyone installing the package obtains working Firebase Admin credentials (FCM, Firestore, Auth) for that project. Additionally, src/controllers/internalTrax.controller.js hardcodes a Lenskart POS API key ('X-Lenskart-API-Key': 'valyoo123') and an account username/password ('tango.eye' / '55eyetango123') used to authenticate to webservice.pos.lenskart.com and central.pos.lenskart.com — this controller is reachable via the package's main entry through its route definitions. A second constant ciphertext is used as a default password for auto-provisioned SPOC users. Installers acquire credentials that authenticate to third-party systems (Google Cloud and Lenskart) not under the installer's control, enabling abuse of those services under the published identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
